sections.paragraph:
ὑπερυψόω 1 aor. ὑπερύψωσα. Pass.: fut. 3 sg. ὑπερύψωθήσεται Da 11:12 v.l.; aor. 2 sg. ὑπερυψώθης Ps 96:9; pf. ptc. ὑπερυψωμένος LXX (Cat. Cod. Astr. XII p. 146, 31; cp. the astrological use of ὑψόω Neugebauer-Hoesen no. 81, 3 ‘the sun in exaltation [ὑψούμενος] in a male and northerly sign’, sim. the noun ὕψωμα [s. p. 199 index]) to raise to a high point of honor, raise, exalt

ⓐ act. raise τινά someone to the loftiest height (Synes., Ep. 79 p. 225a) Phil 2:9 (cp. Ps 96:9, prob. the gods are astral deities; cp. Phil 2:10 ἐπουράνιοι).

ⓑ mid. raise oneself, rise 1 Cl 14:5 (=Ps 36:35).—DELG s.v. ὕψι. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

sim. sim. = similarly

act. act. = active

Synes Synes , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

prob. prob. = probable, probably

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1034
